TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Monthly Switch Backup for BST 10.1.1.252 and 10.1.1.253
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed BST 10.1.1.252 and  10.1.1.253 switch using putty and performed configuration backup. placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network- config-Switch folder. Hence, closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: BST Servers pending for reboot & updates after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machines are now in healthy systems.

bst-hq-app-01, bst-hq-vbr-01

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Huntress portal and identified that the Huntress Agent on the following machines was running an outdated version (v0.14.82): BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01 Connected to each machine via ManageEngine, downloaded and installed the latest Huntress Agent version V0.14.132. Verified in the Huntress portal that all systems are now updated to the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam VBR server having trouble connecting with VSPC portal.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into BST-HQ-VONE server and found the server stopped communication with central VSPC server. Troubleshoot the issue and resetup the connection with VSPC portal. Verify all the services again on the server.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Help
TimeEntry.Notes: Investigated SSL-VPN connectivity issue for user bstremote01 on Windows 11. Verified FortiClient installation (v7.4.3) and reinstalled the application to rule out client-side corruption. Confirmed VPN server reachability via ping and traceroute to vpn.bostongroupwaste.com with no packet loss. Reviewed FortiGate SSL-VPN configuration including listening interface (wan2), custom port 10443, server certificate, and address pool. Verified user account status in Active Directory, confirmed password reset was completed, user was not locked out, and membership in the VPN Users security group was correct. Rebooted the workstation and validated FortiClient connection profile settings matched internal VPN procedure. Reviewed FortiClient and FortiGate logs, confirming repeated SSL-VPN disconnect events without authentication errors. Identified country-based access restriction as a potential cause; added Canada to the SSL-VPN geography whitelist and successfully tested VPN connectivity from the user’s location. After confirmation, removed Canada from the allow list per security controls and informed Jeff of findings. Awaiting approval to permanently whitelist required countries and finalize access rules.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Help
TimeEntry.Notes:
1. Logged into the system. checked and found the VPN connection was giving error.

2. Checked and found drivers and updates were pending, so updated the system and restarted the system. reinstalled & reconfigured the VPN application.

3. Checked the IP address of the system is in which country and found out that the machine is currently in Canada location.

4. logged into the firewall, checked the logs.

5. checked the current setting for the regions Vlan supported and found out that Canada is not added into the approved VLAN list.

6. Created the geographics group Canada in to the firewall and and given the vpn access to it.

7. logged back into the user system and tested the connection. VPN connected successfully.

8. disconnected the system from the vpn, removed the changes done and have informed the user about the changes and asked whether the changes are required.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Help
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged in firewall and whitelisted the Canada location as Jeff requested.

Jeff was able to connect the VPN, closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Gagan with BST is on call for assistance with applications
TimeEntry.Notes:
Gagan called in regarding the Application issue..

Connected to the computer and found that Quickbook and Cietrade application is not stuck and not responding...

So login to server to end the application task....so did that..

Reboot the his system and now he is able to access the Quickbook and Cietrade..

However he said that the computer is working slow..

so need to check the performance issue in after hours.

TimeEntry.Title: Gagan with BST is on call for assistance with applications
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the handover from Surender, Gagan from the Boston Group is facing slowness issues on his computer. I connected to the user's machine, cleared the temp files, prefetch files, and ran disk cleanup. After clean up i send email to user for checking nd we are currently waiting for the user's response.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BST-HQ-APP-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BST-HQ-APP-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Huntress portal and identified that the Huntress Agent on the following machines was running an outdated version (v0.14.82):V0.14.132 on BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE connected to each machine via ManageEngine, downloaded and installed the latest Huntress Agent version V0.14.132. Verified in the Huntress portal that all systems are now updated to the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: BST-HQ-VONE ,BST-HQ-DT-02 / BG77.BostonGroup.local : Found critical at OP manager
TimeEntry.Notes:
• BST-HQ-VONE: The initial error was due to incorrect configuration. The device was deleted and reconfigured with the correct Ethernet interface. A second error was related to the HDD threshold value; the HDD was cleaned as much as possible and re-added. The device is now in a healthy state with no active alerts.

  • BST-HQ-DT-02 / BG77.BostonGroup.local: The device BST-HQ-DT-02 was not found in the system and was verified via MyRemote and MyRMM. The same IP address was associated with BostonGroup.local, which did not have the SNMP service installed. SNMP was downloaded, configured with the correct string value, and added using the proper Ethernet interface. The device is now showing as healthy in OpManager.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machines BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-VBR-01,BST-HQ-RG-01,BST-HQ-RA-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-VBR-01,BST-HQ-RG-01,BST-HQ-RA-01
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20505.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Failed Cloud Veeam backups for BST
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the Veeam backups for BST from the VSPC admin console, found that the cloud backups had failed. Connected to the BST-HQ-VBR-01 server and troubleshot the issue. After that, retried the backup job, and the backups jobs are passed.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage Engine agent upgraded to the latest version 11.5.2600.19 on BST Servers
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.19 W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine ( BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE), Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.19.W) updated:- BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.19.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02) and Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version updated 11.5.2600.19.W - KEVIN2,BG77,BG72
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the My RMM portal, and found the machine KEVIN2,BG77,BG72 running with old version of 
Manage-Engine. So connected to the machine  and then uninstalled the OLD version and downloaded and installed the latest
version (11.5.2600.19.W) of the manage-engine agent. Verified from the portal, device is running with updated agent version.

TimeEntry.Title: BST – Network Detective Report Generated and Updated Under Teams
TimeEntry.Notes: As per assigned task by lead, ND reports was generated successfully for BST Client. Checked and verified the reports are proper. Then uploaded the report to the appropriate client Teams channel for BST. It is now ready for review and take actions on alerts.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam One application configuration error.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into BST-HQ-VONE server and then go through the errors on the Veeam one console and then troubleshoot the errors. Re-setup the inventor and update the connection with esxi servers for client. Once the connection was established then update the alarms again on VONE.

TimeEntry.Title: BST Servers pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE which have not been rebooted. and installed failed patches. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: update missed and failed patche on the BG72 & BG77
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the approved updates and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the pending updates for the systems BG72 & BG77.

Downloaded and manually installed the required driver and BIOS updates. Updated Ethernet Connection (5) I219-LM [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_15E3] to version 12.19.2.66 and upgraded the OptiPlex Micro 7010 BIOS to version 1.33.0.

BG 77 currently pending a reboot to complete the installation; the restart will be performed after business hours to avoid user impact.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BST-HQ-VBR-01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan the machine is now in healthy systems.

Machine : BST-HQ-VBR-01

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated
TimeEntry.Notes:
My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on machine BST-HQ-FS-02,BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BST-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-RG-01
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine BST-HQ-FS-02,BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BST-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-RG-01
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on machine BST-HQ-AD-01,BST-HQ-AD-02,BST-HQ-APP-01,BST-HQ-FS-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine BST-HQ-AD-01,BST-HQ-AD-02,BST-HQ-APP-01,BST-HQ-FS-01
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: BST_HQ_VONE Probe will shutdown shortly.
TimeEntry.Notes: While logging into the OPS Manager admin console, found that the BST probe was down. Logged into the server to check whether the probe was installed, the SNMP service was running, and the community string was added. The probe service was up but still it was showing down in portal. So tried to restart the probe service and refreshed the probe from portal but nothing happened. Then reboot the server, after reboot verified the probe service is up. Then refreshed probe sensor from portal, it is up now and all devices are accessible.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 25.9.9.9533 updated on machine BG72,BG75,BG77,KEVIN2
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine BG72,BG75,BG77,KEVIN2
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.9.9533. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam backup job failed on BST-HQ-VBR-01
TimeEntry.Notes: During routine monitoring of the Veeam backups for BST from the VSPC admin console, I noticed that the cloud backup jobs had failed. I connected to the BST-HQ-VBR-01 server to investigate and attempted to rescan the associated ESXi host, but the scan reported connection issues. To restore communication and stabilize the environment, I rebooted the BST-HQ-VBR-01 server and then performed a fresh rescan of the ESXi host, which completed successfully. After confirming the host was responding normally, I retried the failed backup job and monitored its progress until it completed successfully without further errors.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required -BST-HQ-FS-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BST-HQ-FS-02 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine

TimeEntry.Title: Critical alert for machines BG41,BG52,BG72,Kevin2 on OPS Manager portal
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the OPSManager admin portal, found critical alerts for machines BG41, BG52, BG72, and Kevin2, which were showing as down. Machines BG41 and BG52 were not found on any other portal, so they were removed from the OPSManager portal. For the Kevin2 machine, installed the SNMP service and verified the SNMP credentials — the system is now up and all sensors have resumed operation. For machine BG72, the SNMP service was not installed. Attempted to install it from the backend but was not successful. The machine will need to be rebooted. Will reboot the machine in APD window.

TimeEntry.Title: Call from Gagan Singh regrading VPN issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a call from Gagan Singh reporting that he was unable to log in to the VPN. The connection was getting stuck at 45%, and even after closing and reopening the application, the issue persisted.
I obtained his credentials, connected to the domain controller, and reset his password. After the reset, he was able to connect to the VPN successfully.
He then attempted to connect to his machine using GoToMyPC but encountered an error. I guided him on how to use MyRemote, and after the instructions, everything began working properly.
VPN, MyRemote, and GoToMyPC are now all functioning normally.

TimeEntry.Title: Backups failed on BST-HQ-VBR-01 and BST-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
We have Found three Backups failed on BST-HQ-VBR-01 and BST-HQ-VONE on both the machines.

This issue has been troubleshooted and generated successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BST-HQ-RG-01.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged in to vCenter and found the server BST-HQ-RG-01 is in Disk consolidation state.

checked the snapshots and started consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully.

Then logged into the server and verified all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine on the following machines: BST-HQ-LPT-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, and BST-HQ-RG-01 to version 11.5.2600.19.W.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

  • Updated ManageEngine on the following machines: BST-HQ-LPT-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, and BST-HQ-RG-01 to version 11.5.2600.19.W.

  • Reviewed the My RMM portal and identified that the listed machines were running an outdated version of the ManageEngine agent.

  • Connected to each affected machine, uninstalled the old version, and installed the latest version (11.5.2600.19.W) of the ManageEngine agent.

  • Verified through the portal that all devices are now running the updated agent version.

TimeEntry.Title: Communication stopped from VBR with VSPC central server.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the BST-HQ-VBR server and then check the communication has been broken with central VSPC server. Troubleshoot the issue and re-established the connection and verify the server is communicating with central VSPC server as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BST-HQ-VONE pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan the machine is now in healthy systems.

Machine : BST-HQ-VONE

TimeEntry.Title: Missing and failed patch update on the systems:- BST-HQ-RA-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the manage engine portal and check approved patches for the below mentioned machine then download and installed. connected to the machine through MY RMM and installed the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.


Machine is: - BST-HQ-RA-01


Patch installed:- 
113051 Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) (145.0.3800.70)
43525 Microsoft Edge for chromium business (145.0.3800.70) (x64)
356231 Adobe Acrobat DC (64-bit) (25.001.21223)
43511 Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise Current Channel for x64 2601 of version(19628.20214)

TimeEntry.Title: Missing and failed patch update on the systems:- Kevin2
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the manage engine portal and check approved patches for the below mentioned machine then download and installed. connected to the machine through MY RMM and installed the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.


Machine is: - Kevin2


Patch installed:- 
1319459 Ethernet Connection I217-LM[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_153A] (12.19.2.66)

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches- BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-VBR-01.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the MYRMM portal and then worked on the following machine. Download and install all pending updates on the machine. After that verify all the machines are up and working fine.

BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-VBR-01.

TimeEntry.Title: BG75 : Manually patch update as APD failed to do
TimeEntry.Notes:
Installed the 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework 3.5 and 4.8.1 for Windows.

Installed the latest driver for Ethernet Connection (23) – 1219-LM successfully.

Installed the latest Realtek High Definition Audio driver successfully.

All of these updates have also been reflected in the MyRMM database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Backup location [\\10.1.1.195\BK_BST] is getting low on free disk space (242 GB free of 3.5 TB).
TimeEntry.Notes: Monitor the Veeam backups for BST and found backups were completed with alerts due to low space in the backup storage. To resolve this, I cleaned up the old backups that were older than 14 days and then rescanned the repository. After this backup space has increased 242 GB to 2.3 TB. Now it has sufficient space to complete the backups without any issues.

TimeEntry.Title: BST Servers pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE which have not been rebooted. and installed failed patches. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: BST-HQ-APP-01 System does not sync with the My Remote
TimeEntry.Notes: logged into the my remote portal and noticed that the following machine was not syncing properly with the portal. then logged into the computer through Myrmm to check the my remote services  and restarted it. Rescanned the computer with the portal, and now it syncs properly with the portal

TimeEntry.Title: BST Offsite backups Failed
TimeEntry.Notes: During routine monitoring of the Veeam backups for BST from the VSPC admin console, I noticed that the cloud backup jobs had failed. I connected to the BST-HQ-VBR-01 server to investigate and attempted to rescan the associated ESXi host,I retried the failed backup job and monitored its progress until it completed successfully without further errors.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BST-HQ-AD-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BST-HQ-AD-02 is in Disk consolidation state. checked the snapshots and started consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully. Then logged into the server and verified all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: can anyone please get me this details as well. thank you!
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a request from Rishad regarding the Boston Group’s servers. The request was to provide a list of all servers, including the applications installed on each.

To fulfill the request:

  1. Logged into each server one by one.

  2. Checked the installed applications on each server.

  3. Compiled the details into a sheet.

  4. Sent the completed list to Rishad.

The list includes information on each server's role, installed applications, and any additional details that might help reduce the number of servers.
